---
title: "install.sh CLI"
description: "Top-level installer flags — non-interactive install reference."
---

This page lists every flag accepted by `install.sh` (the script you get from `install.lowkey.run`). Pack-specific flags are covered on each pack's page.

## Flag reference

| Flag | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `-y`, `--yes`, `--non-interactive` | Accept all defaults; skip every prompt. Required for unattended installs. |
| `--simple` | Force simple install mode (pack + profile → auto-configure everything else). |
| `--advanced` | Force advanced install mode (prompt for every parameter). |
| `--pack <name>` | Pre-select agent pack. Valid: `openclaw`, `claude-code`, `codex-cli`, `kiro-cli`, `nemoclaw`, `hermes`, `pi`, `ironclaw`. |
| `--profile <name>` | Pre-select profile. Valid: `builder`, `account_assistant`, `personal_assistant`. |
| `--method <cfn\|terraform\|tf>` | Pre-select deploy method. Default: CFN via CLI. |
| `--kiro-from-secret <id-or-arn>` | Secrets Manager id/arn whose `SecretString` is the Kiro API key (kiro-cli pack, headless mode). |
| `--debug-in-repo` | Dev-only: run the installer from the current working directory instead of downloading. |
| `--test`, `--dry-run` | Run installer end-to-end without provisioning AWS resources. Telemetry tagged `is_test`. |
| `--auto-rename-account-enabled` | Enable auto-rename of AWS account to `Loki-<name>` in headless (`-y`) mode. No-op in interactive mode (user is always prompted). |
| `--disable-account-rename` | Skip account rename entirely. Suppresses the interactive rename prompt too. |
| `--help`, `-h` | Show the help text and exit. |

## Canonical non-interactive invocations

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="OpenClaw builder">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack openclaw --profile builder
    ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Claude Code builder">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack claude-code --profile builder
    ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Codex CLI builder">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack codex-cli --profile builder
    ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Kiro CLI headless builder">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack kiro-cli --profile builder \
      --kiro-from-secret /lowkey/kiro-api-key
    ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="NemoClaw (must be personal)">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack nemoclaw --profile personal_assistant
    ```
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Terraform instead of CloudFormation

```bash
curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
  --pack openclaw --profile builder \
  --method terraform
```

The installer runs Terraform for you under `deploy/terraform/` — `init` → `validate` → `apply -auto-approve`. If you want a review-first flow, use Terraform directly (see [Deploy with Terraform](/reference/terraform)).

## Advanced mode flags

In advanced mode the installer prompts for everything interactively — VPC reuse, instance type, data volume size, region, each security service, etc. Pass `--advanced` to force it even with `-y`:

```bash
curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
  --pack openclaw --profile builder \
  --advanced
```

<Info>
Advanced mode still honors `-y` for prompts that have a clear default; it only asks for the ones that genuinely need a decision.
</Info>

## Connect via SSM

Once the stack is `CREATE_COMPLETE`:

```bash
aws ssm start-session --target <instance-id>
```

SSH is intentionally disabled by default (`SSHAllowedCidr=127.0.0.1/32`). To enable SSH, re-install in advanced mode and set your IP CIDR.

## Environment variables honored

| Variable | Effect |
|---|---|
| `AWS_REGION` / `AWS_DEFAULT_REGION` | Sets the deploy region. |
| `AWS_PROFILE` | Uses the named AWS CLI profile. |
| `DEBUG_IN_REPO=1` | Same as `--debug-in-repo`. |

## Exit codes

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `0` | Install succeeded |
| `1` | Install failed (check stderr) |
| `2` | Bad arguments (parser-level error) |
